title: pyrus cunonioides
intro: Pyrus cunonioides, also known as the native cherry or red ironbark, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae. It is native to Australia and is a small to medium-sized tree with edible fruit and wood that is valued for its durability. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as fever, sore throats, and skin irritations. Pyrus cunonioides can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can provide health benefits like boosting the immune system, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing.
